title: chore: remove dead github_id backfill fallback
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes the unreachable github_id backfill in `onAfterSessionCreate` (auth.ts). GitHub is the only auth provider, so every user row has a `github_id` — verified 0 of 101,653 prod users have NULL `github_id`.
- The fallback (resolve `github_id` from the `account` table + backfill) was a completed-migration bridge that can never execute.
- Replaced with a defensive `if (!githubId) return;` guard; dropped now-unused `account`/`and` imports.
- No behavior change for real users; pure dead-code removal (-31/+6).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the auth hive
gist.blurb: Swept away some old, unused GitHub authentication code. Keeping the hive's internal wiring clean and efficient!
gist.summary: Removed an obsolete GitHub ID backfill fallback in the authentication flow, as all 101k+ production users now have verified IDs.
gist.impact: This internal cleanup removes 31 lines of dead code from the auth flow without changing any user-facing behavior.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee doing some light housekeeping, sweeping up dead leaves (dead code) around a glowing server rack.
